2017-01-26.abstract: The world’s biggest annual human migration is underway as hundreds of millions of Chinese leave the cities to return to their hometowns for the Spring Festival.

2017-01-26.title: Q. and A.: How Trump’s Revival of an Abortion Ban Will Affect Women in Kenya
2017-01-26.abstract: A family planning fellow discusses what President Trump’s ban on aid to groups who offer abortion counseling could mean for women in western Kenya.

2017-01-26.title: Clinics for World’s Vulnerable Brace for Trump’s Anti-Abortion Cuts
2017-01-26.abstract: Health workers say President Trump’s ban on abortion counseling will hurt even those health services that do not involve abortions.

2017-01-27.title: Bana al-Abed Writes Trump: ‘Please Save the Children and People of Syria’
2017-01-27.abstract: The girl, 7, and her mother, Fatemah, posted an open letter to President Trump on Bana’s Twitter account, sharing the message with the hundreds of thousands of users who follow the account.

2017-01-27.title: Stephen P. Cohen, Secret Broker of Mideast Peace Talks, Dies at 71
2017-01-27.abstract: Mr. Cohen arranged the first direct and informal talks between the Israeli government and Yasir Arafat, the Palestine Liberation Organization leader.

2017-01-27.title: In a Corner, President Enrique Peña Nieto of Mexico Punches Back
2017-01-27.abstract: The cancellation of his trip to the United States ended months of diplomacy for a leader caught between his people and an unpredictable, and at times hostile, American president.

2017-01-27.title: Donald Trump and Theresa May: An Odd Couple
2017-01-27.abstract: Britain’s prime minister will meet with the president on Friday. With “Brexit” looming, she needs to show Britons that they have friends beyond Europe.

2017-01-27.title: Surviving Chinese New Year With the Family: A Musical How-To
2017-01-27.abstract: A song, spreading fast online, offers a bit of relief for those dashing home to celebrate the Lunar New Year only to face relentless questions about their lives.

2017-01-28.title: In Iran, Shock and Bewilderment Over Trump Visa Crackdown
2017-01-28.abstract: The new administration’s freeze on visas for a targeted group of countries could provide an early test of its relationship with Tehran.

2017-01-28.title: Fears That Trump’s Visa Ban Betrays Friends and Bolsters Enemies
2017-01-28.abstract: Officials, analysts and citizens across Muslim-majority countries said the order was a sign that President Trump sees Islam itself as the problem.

2017-01-28.title: Trump Administration Holds Off on Issuing U.N. Funding Order
2017-01-28.abstract: A draft order to reduce American funding to the world body, and another that would lead to a review of multinational treaties, have been delayed to allow further study.

2017-01-28.title: How to Make a Chinese Lantern
2017-01-28.abstract: There are more than 30 steps in constructing a lantern for the Chinese New Year. Walk through China’s lantern capital as workers craft the ornament symbolizing luck and happiness.

2017-01-28.title: The Questions That Could Reshape a Worried Europe in 2017
2017-01-28.abstract: The rise of populism, economic threats and the uncertainty of relations with the Trump administration are sources of turbulence for Europe.

2017-01-29.title: In Canada, Justin Trudeau Says Refugees Are Welcome
2017-01-29.abstract: In the wake of President Trump’s order on immigration, the prime minister said his country would accept refugees, “regardless of your faith.”

2017-01-29.title: U.S. Commando Killed in Yemen in Trump’s First Counterterrorism Operation
2017-01-29.abstract: Three others were wounded in the operation against Qaeda militants, which had been deliberated by the Obama administration and was finally authorized by President Trump, military officials said.

2017-01-29.title: In a Pro-‘Brexit’ Corner of Britain, Impatience to Be Done With It
2017-01-29.abstract: Not-so-wealthy Romford prides itself on its Englishness, and is deeply distrustful of an elite many think is angling to double-cross them.

2017-01-29.title: European Leaders Reject Trump’s Refugee Ban as Violating Principles
2017-01-29.abstract: “Open society, pluralism, no discrimination. They are the pillars of Europe,” Italy’s prime minister said, reflecting a stance taken across the Continent.

2017-01-29.title: Netanyahu Says U.S. Embassy ‘Needs to Be’ in Jerusalem
2017-01-29.abstract: The Israeli leader, who had been closemouthed on the issue, did not say President Trump should order an immediate relocation from Tel Aviv.

2017-01-30.title: Benoît Hamon Wins French Socialist Party’s Presidential Nomination
2017-01-30.abstract: Mr. Hamon’s strong showing is unlikely to change widespread assessments that left-leaning candidates have little chance of succeeding in this spring’s general election.

2017-01-30.title: In Face of Trump’s Order, Some Muslim Nations Are Conspicuously Silent
2017-01-30.abstract: Silence in the capitals of some Muslim-majority countries unaffected by the order reflected a lack of solidarity and an enduring uncertainty about Mr. Trump’s intentions.

2017-01-30.title: The Last Hope for Care
2017-01-30.abstract: In Afghanistan's Kunduz Province, Dr. Marzia Salam Yaftali leads the doctors of the last hospital left standing after airstrikes destroyed the area's main trauma center in 2015. She faces her many challenges with kindness and tolerance, she says, to provide care to the people of this war-torn area.

2017-01-30.title: Quebec Mosque Shooting Kills at Least 6, and 2 Suspects Are Arrested
2017-01-30.abstract: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau of Canada condemned the assault, which also injured eight, calling it a “terrorist attack on Muslims.”

2017-01-30.title: Live Briefing: Fatal Shooting at a Quebec City Mosque
2017-01-30.abstract: Armed attackers killed six people and wounded eight others at a mosque on Sunday night in the city of Quebec, the police said.

2017-01-31.title: Syria Reclaims Damascus Water Source From Rebels
2017-01-31.abstract: An insurgent evacuation from the Barada Valley area north of Damascus could signal an end to the water crisis in the capital.

2017-01-31.title: Britons Protest May’s Support of Trump
2017-01-31.abstract: Demonstrators marched in London and other British cities on Monday to voice their displeasure for President Trump and to protest the backing he has received from Prime Minister Theresa May.

2017-01-31.title: Take a Delivery via Beijing’s Streets
2017-01-31.abstract: Ying Lianjun is a critical cog in the world’s largest market for package delivery. China’s e-commerce industry has boomed on the backs of hundreds of thousands of couriers. Follow Mr. Ling on his busy Beijing route.

2017-01-31.title: Papers Offer a Peek at ISIS’ Drones, Lethal and Largely Off-the-Shelf
2017-01-31.abstract: Documents found in Iraq and a recent surge in attacks show how the group has adapted readily accessible technology into a potentially effective new weapon.

2017-01-31.title: Dangerous Fruit: Mystery of Deadly Outbreaks in India Is Solved
2017-01-31.abstract: Researchers had suspected that heat stroke, infections or pesticides were behind a disease that killed about 40 percent of children affected, but it seems lychees were to blame.
